title: DI Prediction All Examples
content:
.di-flex{display:flex;gap:16px;align-items:flex-start}
.di-col{flex:1;min-width:0}
.hi{color:orange;font-weight:bold}
Years to Double Population #031001 极高频 普通
The following graph shows information about years to double population, from 1715 to 1999. 2. According to the graph, we can see that 1715 has the largest number, which is around 544 years. 3. Following that, 1804 has the second largest number, which is around 304 years. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph 1999 has the smallest number, which is around 39 years. 5. It's clear that 1715 has always been higher than 1999. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about years to double population.
Dubai Gold Sales in 2002 #031002 极高频 简单
The following graph shows information about Dubai gold sales in 2002, from January to December. 2. According to the graph, we can see that March has the largest number, which is around 350 million Dirhams. 3. Following that, August has the second largest number, which is around 210 million Dirhams. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph July and September has the smallest number, which is around 130 million Dirhams. 5. It's clear that March has always been higher than July. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about Dubai gold sales in 2002.
Number of Arrests per Year for Using Illegal Drugs
The following graph shows information about the number of arrests per year for using illegal drugs, from 1991 to 2005. 2. According to the graph, we can see that the year 2000 has the largest number, which is around 70. 3. Following that, 1995 and 1997 have the second largest number, which is around 60. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph 1991 has the smallest number, which is around 10. 5. It's clear that the number of arrests fluctuated significantly over the years, with a sharp drop in 2001 to around 15. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about the number of arrests per year for using illegal drugs.
Number of Laws in Brazil 1953-1993
The following graph shows information about the number of laws in Brazil, from 1953 to 1993. 2. According to the graph, we can see that 1993 has the largest number, which is around 2,250. 3. Following that, 1990 has the second largest number, which is around 1,950. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph 1953 has the smallest number, which is around 100. 5. It's clear that the number of laws increased exponentially, especially after 1983. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about the number of laws in Brazil from 1953 to 1993.
S&P/ASX 200
The following graph shows information about the S&P/ASX 200 index, from 10:00 to 2:00. 2. According to the graph, we can see that around 10:30 has the largest number, which is around 3,500. 3. Following that, around 11:30 has the second largest number, which is around 3,490. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph the opening at 10:00 has the smallest number, which is around 3,460. 5. It's clear that the index rose sharply after 10:00, peaked near 3,500, and then declined steadily toward 3,468 by 2:00. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about the S&P/ASX 200 index performance during the trading day.
US Fruit and Vegetable Consumption Trends
The following graph shows information about US fruit and vegetable consumption trends, from 1970 to 2010. 2. According to the graph, we can see that vegetables around 1995 has the largest number, which is around 425 pounds per person per year. 3. Following that, vegetables in 2010 has the second largest number, which is around 395 pounds per person per year. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph fruit in 1970 has the smallest number, which is around 235 pounds per person per year. 5. It's clear that vegetables consumption has always been higher than fruit consumption. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about US fruit and vegetable consumption trends.
Life Expectancy (Years) at Birth by Sex
The following graph shows information about life expectancy at birth by sex, from 1888 to 2013. 2. According to the graph, we can see that females in 2013 has the largest number, which is around 84 years. 3. Following that, males in 2013 has the second largest number, which is around 80 years. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph males in 1888 has the smallest number, which is around 45 years. 5. It's clear that females has always been higher than males. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about life expectancy at birth by sex from 1888 to 2013.
Articles Published per Year by Male and Female University Professors
The following graph shows information about articles published per year by male and female university professors, from 2006 to 2012. 2. According to the graph, we can see that male professors in 2012 has the largest number, which is around 3,300. 3. Following that, male professors in 2008 has the second largest number, which is around 3,200. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph female professors in 2006 has the smallest number, which is around 1,000. 5. It's clear that male professors has always been higher than female professors. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about articles published per year by male and female university professors.
Daily temperature in Beijing China #031024 极高频 简单
The following graph shows information about daily temperature in Beijing China, including daily high and daily low from January to December. 2. According to the graph, we can see that daily high in July has the largest number, which is around 30 degrees Centigrade. 3. Following that, daily low in July has the second largest number, which is around 21 degrees Centigrade. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph daily low in January has the smallest number, which is around minus 10 degrees Centigrade. 5. It's clear that daily high has always been higher than daily low. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about daily temperature in Beijing China.
Population Growth and Growth in Rating Base
The following graph shows information about population growth and growth in rating base, from 2012 to 2022. 2. According to the graph, we can see that growth in rating base in 2018 has the largest number, which is around 2.0%. 3. Following that, growth in rating base in 2022 has the second largest number, which is around 1.85%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph growth in rating base in 2013 has the smallest number, which is around 0.75%. 5. It's clear that population growth was higher before 2016 but growth in rating base surpassed it after 2017. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about population growth and growth in rating base.
Electricity Generation in China by Type 1994-2004
The following graph shows information about electricity generation in China by type, from 1994 to 2004. 2. According to the graph, we can see that conventional thermal in 2004 has the largest number, which is around 1,700 billion kilowatt-hours. 3. Following that, hydroelectric in 2004 has the second largest number, which is around 300 billion kilowatt-hours. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph total generation in 1994 has the smallest number, which is around 900 billion kilowatt-hours. 5. It's clear that conventional thermal has always been higher than hydroelectric. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about electricity generation in China by type from 1994 to 2004.
University and College Students Who Did Educational Activities
The following graph shows information about percent of university and college students who did educational activities by hour of day on weekdays, from 12 am to 11 pm. 2. According to the graph, we can see that full-time students around 11 am has the largest number, which is around 36%. 3. Following that, part-time students around 10 am has the second largest number, which is around 21%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph both groups around 4 am has the smallest number, which is around 0%. 5. It's clear that full-time students has always been higher than part-time students. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about university and college students who did educational activities by hour of day.
Temperature and CO2 for the Last 400,000 Years
The following graph shows information about carbon dioxide and temperature change over the last 400,000 years, from 400,000 years ago to the present. 2. According to the graph, we can see that CO2 at around 300,000 years ago and at present has the largest number, which is around 280-300 ppmv. 3. Following that, temperature at the peaks has the second largest number, which is around +2 to +4 degrees Celsius. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph CO2 at the lowest points has the smallest number, which is around 180 ppmv with temperature around -8 to -10 degrees. 5. It's clear that CO2 levels and temperature changes has always been higher than their respective baselines, showing a strong correlation between the two. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about temperature and CO2 for the last 400,000 years.
Food Price vs Oil Price
The following graph shows information about food price vs oil price, from November 2000 to November 2009. 2. According to the graph, we can see that oil price in mid-2008 has the largest number, which is around $130. 3. Following that, food price index in mid-2008 has the second largest number, which is around 210. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph oil price in 2001 has the smallest number, which is around $20. 5. It's clear that oil price and food price index are strongly correlated, both peaking in 2008 and then declining. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about food price vs oil price.
Projected Population in Australia
The following graph shows information about projected population in Australia, from 2001 to 2101. 2. According to the graph, we can see that Series A in 2101 has the largest number, which is around 63 million. 3. Following that, Series B in 2101 has the second largest number, which is around 45 million. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph all three series in 2001 has the smallest number, which is around 20 million. 5. It's clear that Series A has always been higher than Series B and Series C. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about projected population in Australia.
Annual per Capita Meat Consumption (kg)
The following graph shows information about annual per capita meat consumption in kilograms, from 1961 to 2009. 2. According to the graph, we can see that USA in 2007 has the largest number, which is around 125 kg. 3. Following that, Brazil in 2009 has the second largest number, which is around 85 kg. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph India and Ethiopia has the smallest number, which is around 5 kg throughout the period. 5. It's clear that USA has always been higher than all other countries. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about annual per capita meat consumption across six countries.
Australian Age Group #031053 极高频 简单
The following graph shows information about Australian age group, including males and females. 2. According to the graph, we can see that females in 25-34 has the largest number, which is around 16.5%. 3. Following that, males in 25-34 has the second largest number, which is around 16.2%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph males in 85+ has the smallest number, which is around 0.7%. 5. It's clear that females in 5-14 has always been higher than males in 5-14. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about Australian age group.
Internet Population in Millions of Users #031058 极高频 简单
The following graph shows information about internet population in millions of users, including China, US, Japan, Germany, UK and other countries. 2. According to the graph, we can see that China has the largest number, which is around 180 million users. 3. Following that, US has the second largest number, which is around 165 million users. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph Netherlands has the smallest number, which is around 15 million users. 5. It's clear that China has always been higher than US. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about internet population in millions of users.
The Taxation by Countries - Payroll and Superannuation
The following graph shows information about taxation by countries for payroll and superannuation, from various countries. 2. According to the graph, we can see that Australia has the largest number, which is around 16.6%. 3. Following that, Austria has the second largest number, which is around 16.6%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph Denmark has the smallest number, which is the lowest among the listed countries. 5. It's clear that Australia and Austria has always been higher than other countries including Denmark and the weighted average. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about the taxation by countries for payroll and superannuation.
Unemployment Rates by Ethnic Group and Sex in UK 2001
The following graph shows information about unemployment rates by ethnic group and sex in UK in 2001, from nine ethnic groups including White, Mixed, Indian, Pakistani, Bangladeshi, Other Asian, Black Caribbean, Black African, and Other. 2. According to the graph, we can see that Bangladeshi women has the largest number, which is around 24%. 3. Following that, Pakistani women has the second largest number, which is around 16%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph White men and women has the smallest number, which is around 4-5%. 5. It's clear that Bangladeshi women's unemployment rate has always been higher than all other ethnic groups and sexes. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about unemployment rates by ethnic group and sex in UK 2001.
Earnings and Unemployment Rates by Educational Attainment #031071 极高频 普通
The following graph shows information about earnings and unemployment rates by educational attainment, including unemployment rate and median weekly earnings in 2012. 2. According to the graph, we can see that less than a high school diploma has the largest unemployment rate, which is around 12.4%. 3. Following that, high school diploma has the second largest number, which is around 8.3%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph professional degree has the smallest unemployment rate, which is around 2.1%. 5. It's clear that median weekly earnings for professional degree at $1,735 has always been higher than less than a high school diploma at $471. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about earnings and unemployment rates by educational attainment.
Population of Major European Countries in 1996 and 2007
The following graph shows information about population of major European countries in 1996 and 2007, from Germany, Spain, France, Italy, Poland, United Kingdom and Turkey. 2. According to the graph, we can see that Germany has the largest number, which is around 83 million. 3. Following that, Turkey in 2007 has the second largest number, which is around 75 million. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph Poland has the smallest number, which is around 38 million. 5. It's clear that Germany has always been higher than all other countries. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about population of major European countries in 1996 and 2007.

The following graph shows information about transport modes in a European city, from 1960 to 2000. 2. According to the graph, we can see that car in 2000 has the largest number, which is around 38% of total travellers. 3. Following that, foot in 1960 has the second largest number, which is around 35% of total travellers. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph bike in 2000 has the smallest number, which is around 7% of total travellers. 5. It's clear that car in 2000 has always been higher than car in 1960. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about transport modes in a European city 1960-2000.
Age Percentage in UK #031082 极高频 普通
The following graph shows information about age percentage in UK, from UK 1911, UK 2001 and UK 2011. 2. According to the graph, we can see that 15-64 age group has the largest number, which is around 65%. 3. Following that, 0-14 in UK 1911 has the second largest number, which is around 30%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph 65+ in UK 1911 has the smallest number, which is around 5%. 5. It's clear that 15-64 has always been higher than 0-14 and 65+. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about age percentage in UK.
Beginning of flow chart for quiz #031086 极高频 简单
The following graph shows information about beginning of flow chart for quiz, including Slide 1 to Slide 5. 2. According to the graph, we can see that the first step is Slide 1, click to start quiz. 3. Following that, the second step is Slide 2, Question 1. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph, the third step is click true or click false. 5. It's clear that the last steps are Slide 3 correct or Slide 4 wrong, then click to go to Slide 5, Question 2. 6. We can see that there is a decision process in the picture, which is interactive and structured. 7. In general, this graph is very informative about beginning of flow chart for quiz.
Age Percentage by Gender
The following graph shows information about age percentage by gender, including females and males from age 1-15 to 85+. 2. According to the graph, we can see that females in age 1-15 has the largest number, which is around 15.5%. 3. Following that, males in age 1-15 has the second largest number, which is around a similar percentage. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph females in age 85+ has the smallest number, which is around 0.4%. 5. It's clear that younger age groups has always been higher than older age groups. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about age percentage by gender.
Languages Have Influenced English
The following graph shows information about languages that have influenced English, including several languages shown in different colors. 2. According to the graph, we can see that the red section has the largest portion, which is around the biggest slice of the pie chart. 3. Following that, the blue section has the second largest portion, which is around the second biggest slice. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph the yellow section has the smallest portion, which is around the tiniest slice of the pie chart. 5. It's clear that the red section has always been larger than all other sections. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about languages that have influenced English.
Approximate Composition of the Air
The following graph shows information about approximate composition of the air, including nitrogen, oxygen and other gases. 2. According to the graph, we can see that nitrogen has the largest portion, which is around 79%. 3. Following that, oxygen has the second largest portion, which is around 20%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph other gases has the smallest portion, which is around 1%, including carbon dioxide at 0.03%, argon and water vapour. 5. It's clear that nitrogen has always been higher than oxygen and other gases. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about approximate composition of the air.
Pet Expenditure in US #031133 极高频 简单
The following graph shows information about pet expenditure in US, including food and litter, vet care and wellbeing, services grooming and boarding, and pet purchase. 2. According to the graph, we can see that vet care and wellbeing has the largest number, which is around 47%. 3. Following that, food and litter has the second largest number, which is around 41%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph pet purchase has the smallest number, which is around 2%. 5. It's clear that vet care and wellbeing has always been higher than food and litter. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about pet expenditure in US.
Enrollments on Six Courses
The following graph shows information about enrollments on six courses at a local education college in the UK, from 2010 and 2012. 2. According to the graph, we can see that Social Studies in 2010 has the largest number, which is around 27%. 3. Following that, Nursing in 2010 has the second largest number, which is around 26.7%. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph Carpentry in 2012 has the smallest number, which is around 5%. 5. It's clear that Nursing and Social Studies has always been higher than Carpentry and Sports Science. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about enrollments on six courses.
Populations of Countries of the European Union in 1998 and 2007
The following graph shows information about populations of countries of the European Union in 1998 and 2007 by percentage, including Germany, France, United Kingdom, Italy, Spain, Poland and all other countries. 2. According to the graph, we can see that all other countries (21) has the largest number, which is around 29.4% in 1998 and 29.7% in 2007. 3. Following that, Germany has the second largest number, which is around 17.1% in 1998 and 16.6% in 2007. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph Poland has the smallest number, which is around 8.0% in 1998 and 7.7% in 2007. 5. It's clear that Germany has always been higher than France and United Kingdom. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about populations of countries of the European Union in 1998 and 2007.
Size of Nine Planets #031141 极高频 极限预测 困难
The following graph shows information about size of nine planets, including Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, Earth and Pluto. 2. According to the graph, we can see that Jupiter has the largest size, which is the biggest planet. 3. Following that, Saturn has the second largest size, which is slightly smaller than Jupiter. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph Pluto has the smallest size, which is the tiniest object in the picture. 5. It's clear that Jupiter has always been bigger than all other planets. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about size of nine planets.
Typing Postures #031142 极高频 困难
The following graph shows information about typing postures, including right and wrong postures. 
According to the graph we can see that on the left side of the picture, the top left picture shows the correct typing posture in the top view., which is straight and aligned with the keyboard. 
Following that, in the middle of the picture, the bottom left picture shows the correct typing posture in the side view., which is clearly illustrated. 
Based on the information we can get from the graph on the right side of the picture, The top right picture shows the wrong typing posture in the top view., which is bent and angled incorrectly. 
It's clear that at the bottom of the picture, the bottom right picture shows the wrong typing posture in the side view., which is also showing right and wrong positions. 
In general, this graph is very informative about typing postures.

The following graph shows information about adults healthy BMI chart, including height from 1.36 to 2.00 metres and weight from 45 to 110 kilos. 2. According to the graph, we can see that extreme obese has the largest area, which is around the top left corner in red. 3. Following that, obese has the second largest area, which is around the upper middle section in pink. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph under weight has the smallest area, which is around the bottom right corner in green. 5. It's clear that healthy weight has always been between under weight and over weight. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about adults healthy BMI chart.
How Solar Yard Lights Work #031145 极高频 困难
The following graph shows information about how solar yard lights work, including several components. 2. According to the graph we can see that on the left side of the picture, there is labels for each component, which is clearly marked. 3. Following that, in the middle of the picture, there is glass cover, solar cells and photoresistor, which is located at the top part of the light. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph on the right side of the picture, there is battery, controller board and LED, which is the internal components in the middle part. 5. It's clear that at the bottom of the picture, there is lamp cover, which is the base of the solar yard light. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about how solar yard lights work.

The following graph shows information about student accommodation of Southampton University, including a floor plan of 5.40m by 6.80m. 2. According to the graph we can see that on the left side of the picture, there is a kitchen area and a single bed, which is located next to each other. 3. Following that, in the middle of the picture, there is a desk and a bathroom, which is in the central area of the room. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph on the right side of the picture, there is a sofa and a wardrobe, which is placed along the right wall. 5. It's clear that at the bottom of the picture, there is another bed and a washing machine, which is in the lower section of the accommodation. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about student accommodation of Southampton University.
Food Pyramid #031148 极高频 极限预测 困难
The following graph shows information about food pyramid, including different food groups from top to bottom. 2. According to the graph we can see that on the left side of the picture, there is milk, cheese and eggs, and bread and cereals, which is in the middle and lower layers of the pyramid. 3. Following that, in the middle of the picture, there is junk food, sweets and sugar drinks at the top, which is the smallest portion we should eat the least. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph on the right side of the picture, there is meat, poultry, sausage and fish, and fruits and vegetables, which is in the middle and lower layers. 5. It's clear that at the bottom of the picture, there is water, which is the most essential and largest portion at the base. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about food pyramid.
The Graduation Laboratory
The following graph shows information about the graduation laboratory, including offices, storage, toilets, animal sciences, plant sciences and computer station. 2. According to the graph we can see that on the left side of the picture, there is three offices, a storage room and toilets, which is located in the upper left area. 3. Following that, in the middle of the picture, there is a staircase and a corridor, which is connecting different sections of the laboratory. 4. Based on the information we can get from the graph on the right side of the picture, there is a computer station with multiple workstations, which is arranged around a central table. It's clear that at the bottom of the picture, there is animal sciences and plant sciences, which is two large rooms at the lower level. 5. In general, this picture is very informative about the graduation laboratory.
Library Plan #031151 极高频 困难
The following graph shows information about library plan, including 5 functional areas. 2. According to the graph we can see that on the left side of the picture, there is area 1 in pink and area 4 in yellow, which is on the upper and lower left. 3. Following that, in the middle of the picture, there is area 2 in blue and area 5 in brown with an entrance between them, which is in the central part of the library. 4. According to the graph we can see that on the left side of the picture, there is a meander in stage A, which is a curved river bend. 3. It's clear that at the bottom of the picture, there is stage B showing an oxbow lake and a new channel, which is formed after the river cuts through the neck. 6. In general, this graph is very informative about oxbow lake formation.

The following graph shows information about the eatwell plate, including fruit and vegetables, bread rice potatoes pasta, meat fish eggs beans, milk and dairy foods, and foods high in fat and sugar. 2. According to the graph, we can see that fruit and vegetables has the largest portion, which is around the biggest green section of the plate. 3. Following that, bread, rice, potatoes, pasta and other starchy foods has the second largest portion, which is around the orange section. 4. title: RL/SST/SGD Template
content:
.pronoun {
  color: pink;
  font-weight: bold;
  font-style: italic;
}


.blank {
  color: orange;
  font-weight: bold;
  font-style: italic;
}


::: details Deprecated Tempalte

The Unified Template 

::: tabs

@tab 6 lines

The [lecture/discussion] provides information on  (main topic/theme) and highlights several key aspects.

To begin with, the [speaker(s)/participants] introduce  (first key idea / general concept) and give some background details.

Next, they discuss  (second key idea / development / change) and explain the reasons or ideas related to it.

Furthermore, they illustrate and evaluate  (example / case + opinion or viewpoint) with supporting details.

Finally, an important point about  (key takeaway / conclusion point) is emphasized toward the end.

In conclusion, the [lecture/discussion] offers valuable insights into  (overall summary of topic) and is overall informative.

@tab 7 lines

The [lecture/discussion] provides information on  (main topic/theme) and highlights several key aspects.

To begin with, the [speaker(s)/participants] introduce  (first key idea / general concept) and give some background details.

Next, they discuss  (second key idea / development / change) and explain the reasons or ideas related to it.

Furthermore, they illustrate  (specific example / case / situation) with examples or supporting details.

In addition, different views on  (controversial point / opinion / issue) are mentioned, including agreement or disagreement.

Finally, an important point about  (key takeaway / warning / conclusion point) is emphasized toward the end.

In conclusion, the [lecture/discussion] offers valuable insights into  (overall summary of topic) and is overall informative.

:::

:::

--- 

RL/SST Tempalte
The lecture gives information on [Topic] and highlights several key points.

Firstly, the speaker talks about [first key idea / general concept].

Later, the speaker gives information about  [second key idea / development / change] and explains the reasons behind it.

Furthermore, the speaker illustrates the point with an example of  [specific example / case / situation].

In the end, they emphasize an important note on  [key takeaway / warning / conclusion point].

In conclusion, the lecture offers valuable insights into [Topic] and is very informative.



SGD Template
The discussion involves several people/students/teachers talking about [Topic].

First of all, the first speaker talks about [first key idea / general concept].
 Later, he/she provides more information about [details, reasons, or explanation] and explains the reasons behind it.
 Furthermore, the first speaker illustrates the point with an example of [specific example, case, or scenario supporting the idea].
 In the end, the first speaker emphasizes an important note on [key takeaway, conclusion point, or caution].

Following the first speaker, the second speaker turns to [second key idea / general concept].
 Later, he/she provides more information about [details, reasons, or explanation] and explains the reasons behind it.
 Furthermore, the first speaker illustrates the point with an example of [specific example, case, or scenario supporting the idea].
 In the end, the first speaker emphasizes an important note on [key takeaway, conclusion point, or caution].

As for the third speaker, he/she shares view on [the idea].
 Later, he/she provides more information about [details, reasons, or explanation] and explains the reasons behind it.
 Furthermore, the first speaker illustrates the point with an example of [specific example, case, or scenario supporting the idea].In the end, the first speaker emphasizes an important note on [key takeaway, conclusion point, or caution].

In conclusion, the discussion focuses on [Topic] and examines [overall ideas, issues, or key points discussed by all speakers] throughout the dialogue.

  .map(([path, mod]) => {
    const filename = path.split('/').pop();
    const match = filename.match(/^(\d+)\.(.+)\.\w+$/);
    if (!match) return null;
    const num = parseInt(match[1]);
    const title = match[2].replace(/_/g, ' ');
    const src = typeof mod.default === 'string' ? mod.default : mod.default?.src;
    const category = categoryMap[num] || 'image';
    const description = descriptionMap[num] || '';
    return { src, title: , num, category, description };
  })
  .filter(Boolean)
  .sort((a, b) => a.num - b.num);

title: Writing Examples
content:
法律改变人的行为 #101003 极高频 极限预测

::: tabs

@tab Question

Some people think human behaviors can be changed by laws, while others think laws have little effect. What is your opinion.
一些人认为法律可以限制人的行为，另一些人认为法律没有什么作用。你的意见是什么?

::: details 观点1：可以限制
Personally speaking, human behaviors can be changed by laws.
 论点1：防止犯罪Laws can prevent people from taking part in crime.
   参考论证1：法律 – 惩罚 – 避免犯罪[legislations] – [punishments and penalties] – [reduced criminal behavior]
   参考论证2：法律 – 定义犯罪行为 – 避免犯法[rules and regulations] – [definition of criminal behaviors] – [the avoidance of crime]
 论点2：正确价值观Laws can help shape citizens’ sense of morality and influence their behavior.
   参考论证1：法律 – 定义道德标准 – 道德行为[laws and legislations] – [moral standards] – [ethical conduct]
   参考论证2：法律 – 清晰的行为准则 – 遵守法律的行为[rules and regulations] – [clear behavioral boundaries] – [lawful compliance]
 论点3：承担社会责任Laws can guide people to assume their social responsibilities.
   参考论证1：法律 – 建立社会责任感 – 对社会负责的行为[laws and legislations] – [a sense of public duty] – [responsible conduct]
   参考论证2：法律 – 法律义务 – 实现法律责任[rules and regulations] – [legal obligations] – [the fulfilment of civic duties]

:::

::: details 观点2：不能限制
Personally speaking, laws have little effect on human behaviors.
 论点1：法律约束无法限制内在动机Legal control have limited capacity to regulate people’s internal motivations and behaviors.
   参考论证1：法律 – 建立社会责任感 – 对社会负责的行为[laws and legislation] – [external constraints] – [limited influence on personal choices]
   参考论证2：法律 – 法律义务 – 实现法律责任[rules and regulation] – [limited fear of punishiment] – [temporary compliance]
 论点2：法律缺乏执行力Law’s limited enforcement reduces the ability to regulate behavior.参考论证1：法律 – 执行力有限 – 震慑作用低[laws and legislation] – [enforcement gaps] – [low deterrent effect]参考论证2：法律 – 监管能力不足 – 规避合法行为[rules and regulation] – [weak supervision] – [rule avoidance]论点3：法律不能预防犯罪行为Laws are unable to prevent legal violation.参考论证1：法律 – 以惩罚为主的机制 – 预防作用有限[laws and legislation] – [punishment-based measures] – [weak preventive effect]参考论证2：法律 – 事后型执行方式 – 行为预防能力不足[rules and regulation] – [reactive enforcement] – [limited behavioral prevention]

:::

@tab Answer

 Paragraph 1 — Introduction

  In today’s rapidly evolving world, many issues continue to attract public attention and debate. One such issue is whether human behaviors can be changed by laws, or if laws have little effect. While different people hold different opinions regarding this matter, my view is that human behaviors can indeed be changed by laws. This essay will discuss the key aspects of the topic and explain why this perspective is reasonable through logical arguments and relevant examples.

 Paragraph 2 — First Main Argument

  To begin with, one major aspect of legal influence is its ability to prevent people from taking part in crime. This means that through legislations, punishments, and penalties, the legal system can lead to reduced criminal behavior. For instance, rules and regulations that provide a clear definition of criminal behaviors help citizens ensure the avoidance of crime, which clearly illustrates the importance of this factor. Therefore, this argument strongly supports the idea that laws serve as an effective deterrent.

 Paragraph 3 — Second Main Argument

  Secondly, another significant consideration is that laws can help shape citizens' sense of morality and influence their behavior. This is because laws and legislations define moral standards which lead to ethical conduct. For example, rules and regulations establishing clear behavioral boundaries result in lawful compliance demonstrates how this aspect affects the issue. Even though some may argue that legal controls have limited capacity to regulate internal motivations, the overall evidence suggests that laws guide people to assume their social responsibilities.

 Paragraph 4 — Conclusion

  In conclusion, the impact of laws on human behavior is a complex issue with multiple perspectives. After examining the main arguments and supporting evidence, it is clear that laws are effective in preventing crime and shaping moral standards. Ultimately, adopting a thoughtful and balanced understanding of this topic will help individuals and society make better decisions in the future.
+1

:::

员工参与决策的利弊 #101004 极高频 极限预测

::: tabs

@tab Question

In some companies, some employers involve employees in the decision-making process of products and services. What are the advantages and disadvantages of such a policy?
有些雇主会让员工参与产品和服务的决策过程。这种政策的优点和缺点是什么?

::: details 观点：既有优点，也有缺点
Personally speaking, I firmly believe that there are both advantages and disadvantages of such a policy.
 论点1：增加员工忠诚度Employees' participation in the decision-making process of products and services can result in high employee loyalty.
   参考论证1：员工参与决策 – 工作参与度 – 归属感[employees making decisions] – [more engagement in work] – [a sense of belonging]
   参考论证2：员工参与决策 – 动力和热情 – 忠诚度[involving employees in the decision-making process] – [their motivation and passion] – [higher employment loyalty]
 论点2：提高工作表现Involving employees in the decision-making process of products and services will lead to better business performance.
   参考论证1：员工参与决策 – 更好的商业建议 – 更高的公司收入[employees making decisions] – [better business suggestions] – [more business revenue]
   参考论证2：员工参与决策 – 满足顾客需求 – 顾客认可度[involving employees in the decision-making process] – [meeting clients' demand] – [customers' recognition]

:::

::: details 缺点
 论点1：产生上下级矛盾Employees participating in the decision-making process may create conflicts between employees and managers.
   参考论证1：员工参与决策 – 意见不同和争执 – 老板员工之间的冲突[staff participation] – [disagreement or arguments] – [conflicts among employees and employers]
   参考论证2：员工参与决策 – 不同意见 – 管理层和员工之间关系紧张[staff engagement in decision making] – [different opinions] – [tensions between management and employees]
 论点2：降低工作效率Employees engaged in the decision-making process will cause a decrease in working efficiency.
   参考论证1：员工参与决策 – 复杂的协商流程 – 浪费时间[employee involvement in decision making] – [complex negotiation procedures] – [waste of time]
   参考论证2：员工参与决策 – 冗长的讨论时间 – 拖慢工作进度[employee engagement in the decision process] – [longer discussion time] – [slower work progress]

:::

@tab Answer

 Paragraph 1 — Introduction

  In today's rapidly evolving world, many issues continue to attract public attention and debate. One such issue is whether involving employees in the decision-making process of products and services brings more advantages or disadvantages. While different people hold different opinions regarding this matter, my view is that there are both advantages and disadvantages of such a policy. This essay will discuss the key aspects of the topic and explain why this perspective is reasonable through logical arguments and relevant examples.

 Paragraph 2 — Advantages

  To begin with, one major advantage of involving employees in decision-making is that it can result in high employee loyalty. This means that employees making decisions can lead to more engagement in work, which further results in a sense of belonging. For instance, involving employees in the decision-making process can bring about their motivation and passion, ultimately leading to higher employment loyalty, which clearly illustrates the positive impact. Therefore, this argument strongly supports the idea that employee participation enhances workplace commitment.

 Paragraph 3 — Disadvantages

  However, there are also notable disadvantages. One significant concern is that employees participating in the decision-making process may create conflicts between employees and managers. This is because staff participation can result in disagreement or arguments, which leads to conflicts among employees and employers. For example, staff engagement in decision making can contribute to different opinions, bringing about tensions between management and employees demonstrates the negative impact. Despite these drawbacks, a balanced understanding of both sides remains essential for employee involvement in decision-making.

 Paragraph 4 — Conclusion

  In conclusion, employee participation in organizational decision-making is a complex issue with both benefits and drawbacks. After examining the main arguments and supporting evidence, it is clear that while it increases employee loyalty, it may also create workplace conflicts. Ultimately, adopting a thoughtful and balanced understanding of this topic will help individuals and society make better decisions in the future.
+1

:::

书面考试是否有效评估学习成果 #101005 极高频 极限预测

::: tabs

@tab Question

The formal written examination can be a valid method to assess students' learning. To what extent do you agree or disagree? Use your own experience.
正式的笔试是评估学生学习成绩的有效方法。你在多大程度上同意或不同意? 运用你自己的经验。

::: details 观点1：是有效方法
Personally speaking, I firmly believe that a formal written examination can be a valid method to assess students' learning.
 论点1：准确评估知识掌握程度A formal written examination can lead to a clear measurement of knowledge mastery.
